deep_dive.title: Vision Token Manipulation Attacks on Cloud-Edge Inference of Large Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Cloud-edge Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM) inference enables efficient deployment by splitting computation between edge devices and cloud servers. In this process, intermediate vision tokens are transmitted from the edge to the cloud over a communication link, thereby exposing a new attack surface. We study vision token manipulation attack (VTM-Attack) under a black-box man-in-the-middle setting, where an adversary intercepts and manipulates a subset of transmitted vision tokens under a budget constraint. We propose four naïve attack strategies and an optimization-based token selection method. Experiments on 6 state-of-the-art LVLMs (3B-72B) across 4 benchmarks show that manipulating only 10\% of vision tokens can reduce accuracy by up to 88.31\%. These results reveal a critical vulnerability in cloud-edge LVLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Token manipulation attack on heterogeneous cloud-edge LVLM system
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对云边异构LVLM系统的令牌操纵攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对云边异构LVLM系统的令牌操纵攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对云边异构LVLM系统的令牌操纵攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Demonstrates vision token manipulation attacks on cloud-edge large vision-language model inference, showing that manipulating 10% of tokens can drastically reduce accuracy, exposing security vulnerabilities in heterogeneous computing pipelines.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对云边协同大视觉语言模型推理提出视觉令牌操纵攻击，在中间传输过程中，攻击者以黑盒中间人方式操纵少量视觉令牌，仅需10%令牌即可显著降低模型精度。实验覆盖6种主流模型和4个基准，揭示出异构计算部署中云边通信链路的新攻击面。该研究首次系统分析了LVLM推理在分割计算场景下的安全挑战，为异构系统安全防御提供了重要警示，贡献于CPU/GPU环境中的安全加固研究。

deep_dive.title: HiFuzz: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Semantic-Aware and Adaptive CPU Fuzzing
deep_dive.abstract: Modern processor verification struggles to reach deep architectural states due to the inefficiencies of traditional mutation-based fuzzing. We propose HiFuzz, a novel hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that replaces mutation with a structured, two-layer generation process: a Program Agent for global layout and a Basic Block Agent for precise instruction filling. To overcome reward sparsity, HiFuzz integrates an adaptive coverage reward mechanism and a semantic-aware basic block encoder providing intrinsic feedback. Extensive evaluations on three real-world RISC-V cores demonstrate that HiFuzz significantly outperforms state-of-the-art fuzzers in coverage and bug detection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: CPU fuzzing with hierarchical reinforcement learning for vulnerability discovery
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于分层强化学习的CPU模糊测试以发现漏洞
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于分层强化学习的CPU模糊测试以发现漏洞
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于分层强化学习的CPU模糊测试以发现漏洞
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes HiFuzz, a hierarchical RL-based fuzzing framework that generates structured test programs for CPU verification, achieving higher coverage and bug detection on RISC-V cores compared to traditional mutation-based fuzzers.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统基于变异的处理器模糊测试难以达到深层架构状态。HiFuzz提出分层强化学习框架，以程序层和基本块层两级代理生成结构化测试程序，结合自适应覆盖奖励和语义感知编码，提升RISC-V处理器的漏洞发现能力。实验显示，在三个真实RISC-V核心上，代码覆盖和Bug检测数量均显著优于现有模糊测试工具。该方法为硬件安全验证提供了高效的动态分析手段，对处理器信任建设具有重要意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 传统基于变异的处理器模糊测试难以达到深层架构状态。HiFuzz提出分层强化学习框架，以程序层和基本块层两级代理生成结构化测试程序，结合自适应覆盖奖励和语义感知编码，提升RISC-V处理器的漏洞发现能力。实验显示，在三个真实RISC-V核心上，代码覆盖和Bug检测数量均显著优于现有模糊测试工具。该方法为硬件安全验证提供了高效的动态分析手段，对处理器信任建设具有重要意义。

deep_dive.title: KS-CFA: Control-Flow Attestation via Symbolic Replay Against Control-Flow Bending Attacks
deep_dive.abstract: Control-flow attestation (CFA) enables a remote entity to verify program execution on a target device by monitoring control-flow behaviour at runtime. However, control-flow bending (CFB) attacks remain difficult to detect, where an adversary steers execution along legal edges of the program's control-flow graph by corrupting branch flags, loop counters, and other runtime data. Existing solutions impose significant drawbacks: they require enumerating vast measurement spaces, cover only a reduced subset of attacks, or rely on low-level hardware modifications. In this work, we present KS-CFA, a new CFA scheme that detects CFB attacks across four transfer types (indirect calls, conditional and indirect jumps, and returns) without those costs. To this end, we combine symbolic execution and selective identification of input-sourced control-flow dependent variables: a strict subset of control-flow-relevant state whose values are directly read from external input. The proving device records, inside a trusted execution environment (TEE), a control-flow trace and the external inputs that determine relevant run-time variables. The verifier then replays the reported path through single-path symbolic execution, predicting each transfer and localising divergences that signal an attack. We implement and evaluate KS-CFA using the RISC-V Keystone TEE and Embench-IoT on QEMU and a Rocket-based FPGA platform (NiteFury II). Prover-side overhead relative to unattested execution ranges from 6.8-20.5x on QEMU and 6.7-32.2x on the FPGA, and verification requires no path or value enumeration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: runtime control-flow attestation with symbolic replay against control-flow bending attacks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过符号重放的运行时控制流证明对抗控制流弯曲攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过符号重放的运行时控制流证明对抗控制流弯曲攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过符号重放的运行时控制流证明对抗控制流弯曲攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces KS-CFA, a control-flow attestation scheme using symbolic replay to detect control-flow bending attacks across all transfer types without hardware changes, improving remote verification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对控制流弯曲攻击难以全面检测的问题，提出KS-CFA方案，通过符号重放技术在不依赖硬件修改的条件下，实现对间接调用、条件/间接跳转和返回四种转移类型的攻击检测。该方法弥补了现有方案覆盖不全的缺陷，增强了远程设备执行的可信验证能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对控制流弯曲攻击难以全面检测的问题，提出KS-CFA方案，通过符号重放技术在不依赖硬件修改的条件下，实现对间接调用、条件/间接跳转和返回四种转移类型的攻击检测。该方法弥补了现有方案覆盖不全的缺陷，增强了远程设备执行的可信验证能力。

deep_dive.title: Mini-Programs, Mega-Problems: Unveiling OAuth-based Authentication Misuses in Mini-Programs via Dynamic Analysis
deep_dive.abstract: Mini-programs have become a dominant paradigm for lightweight application deployment within super apps such as WeChat. To support seamless integration, super apps provide OAuth mechanisms for user login. However, improper integration of OAuth-based Authentication (OBA) flows by third-party developers can lead to critical security flaws. In this paper, we discover three new types of runtime OBA misuses that differ from prior static-code-based studies, enabling attackers to impersonate victims. To assess their real-world impact, we design and implement MINIAUTH, the first analysis framework for systematically analyzing OBA misuse at scale. MINIAUTH automatically pinpoints the OBA login page of a mini-program, executes the workflow dynamically, and analyzes its runtime behaviors. This enables it to handle obfuscated mini-programs and uncover vulnerabilities that existing approaches cannot detect. Applying MINIAUTH to 44,273 WeChat and 2,721 Baidu mini-programs, we uncover 1,834 misuse cases, including critical logic flaws that enable client-side identity forgery via exposed credentials and authentication bypass through static or plaintext identifiers. Our cross-platform evaluation further shows that such misuses are not confined to a single ecosystem but consistently appear across different mini-program platforms. We also identify a cryptographic design flaw in Baidu's OBA APIs that allows brute-forcing of session keys. We responsibly disclosed our findings to the developers and platforms, receiving acknowledgments and assigned CNVD/CNNVD IDs. These results underscore the need for more robust developer guidance and enhanced platform-level safeguards.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Uses dynamic analysis to detect runtime OAuth authentication misuses in mini-programs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过动态分析检测小程序中的运行时 OAuth 认证误用
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过动态分析检测小程序中的运行时 OAuth 认证误用
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过动态分析检测小程序中的运行时 OAuth 认证误用
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Discovers novel runtime OAuth misuse in mini-programs and proposes MINIAUTH, a dynamic analysis framework for large-scale detection, revealing widespread vulnerabilities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文发现小程序中三种新的运行时 OAuth 认证误用，并设计 MINIAUTH 动态分析框架，自动定位登录页面、执行流程分析，在大规模检测中发现严重安全问题，为小程序生态提供了有效的安全分析工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文发现小程序中三种新的运行时 OAuth 认证误用，并设计 MINIAUTH 动态分析框架，自动定位登录页面、执行流程分析，在大规模检测中发现严重安全问题，为小程序生态提供了有效的安全分析工具。

deep_dive.title: Coded Task Offloading for Fluid Computing: A Privacy-Aware Approach under D2D Networks
deep_dive.abstract: Fluid Computing aims to support distributed applications execution across heterogeneous cloud, edge, and device resources, motivating task execution mechanisms that adapt to dynamic and privacy-sensitive environments under runtime conditions. In this context, current task offloading schemes rarely address privacy risks and information leakage under adversarial execution settings; furthermore, most coded computing proposals focus on straggler mitigation without considering system-level objectives such as energy awareness. This paper proposes a coded task offloading scheme for D2D networks under stochastic task arrivals and queue-based dynamics. The proposal combines task offloading techniques with linear secret sharing schemes, where tasks are encoded into redundant shares to support threshold-based recovery, straggler mitigation, and privacy preservation while enhancing system performance. Then, we formulate a privacy-aware offloading problem that jointly optimizes delay and energy while penalizing the theoretical privacy leakage of coded tasks under noisy leakage observations. The problem is solved using a branch-and-bound solver alongside a lightweight heuristic scheduler, both of which are evaluated through a discrete-event simulator. Results show that coded offloading improves the delay--energy trade-off with respect to classical full and parallel offloading schemes, while the heuristic achieves near-optimal performance, outperforming baseline and state-of-the-art solvers. The results also show how privacy leakage penalties reshape offloading decisions, exposing an inherent delay--energy--privacy trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a privacy-aware coded task offloading scheme for heterogeneous computing environments
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向异构计算环境的隐私感知编码任务卸载方案
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出面向异构计算环境的隐私感知编码任务卸载方案
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出面向异构计算环境的隐私感知编码任务卸载方案
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses privacy risks in task offloading for heterogeneous fluid computing by combining task offloading with linear secret sharing, achieving privacy protection under adversarial execution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对异构流体计算中任务卸载的隐私风险，该文提出一种结合线性秘密共享的编码任务卸载方案，在随机任务到达和队列动态下实现隐私保护，同时考虑了能量感知，为资源异构环境下的安全计算提供了新方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对异构流体计算中任务卸载的隐私风险，该文提出一种结合线性秘密共享的编码任务卸载方案，在随机任务到达和队列动态下实现隐私保护，同时考虑了能量感知，为资源异构环境下的安全计算提供了新方法。

deep_dive.title: Evaluating Endpoint Detection Robustness Against Genetic Algorithm Driven Code Transformations
deep_dive.abstract: Post-compromise test variants are widely used in controlled security evaluation and endpoint robustness benchmarking. However, modern Antivirus (AV) and Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) systems increasingly combine signature- and behavior-based detection, challenging the reliability of conventional detection pipelines under adaptive variation. This study introduces ShellForge, a Genetic Algorithm (GA)-driven framework that evolves post-compromise variants representative of remote command execution to generate functionally equivalent variants for systematic detection evaluation. ShellForge applies syntactic transformations, encoding schemes, and structural permutations guided by a multi-objective fitness function informed by AV and EDR detection feedback. We compare ShellForge against representative baseline transformation frameworks under identical sandbox configurations. Our findings highlight measurable robustness gaps in baseline signature- and behavior-oriented detection pipelines under controlled variant generation. In addition, we propose a reproducible benchmark for endpoint detection robustness evaluation, motivating the need for robustness-aware defensive monitoring and behavioral correlation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evolved code variants testing endpoint detection robustness
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 进化代码变体测试端点检测鲁棒性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 进化代码变体测试端点检测鲁棒性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 进化代码变体测试端点检测鲁棒性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ShellForge framework uses genetic algorithm to evolve functionally equivalent post-compromise variants, challenging signature- and behavior-based detection systems to evaluate and improve endpoint security.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现代防病毒和端点检测响应系统日益结合签名与行为检测的现状，ShellForge框架采用遗传算法驱动生成功能等效的后渗透变体，通过多目标适应度引导语法变换、编码模式和结构置换，系统评估检测管道的鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法能有效暴露检测弱点，为提升端点防御提供了先进测试手段。该研究为软件漏洞利用防御中的检测评估提供了新工具，推动防护体系的持续改进。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现代防病毒和端点检测响应系统日益结合签名与行为检测的现状，ShellForge框架采用遗传算法驱动生成功能等效的后渗透变体，通过多目标适应度引导语法变换、编码模式和结构置换，系统评估检测管道的鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法能有效暴露检测弱点，为提升端点防御提供了先进测试手段。该研究为软件漏洞利用防御中的检测评估提供了新工具，推动防护体系的持续改进。

quick_skim.title: Agentic SABRE: An Uncertainty-Aware Neuro-Symbolic Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Ransomware Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Ransomware has evolved into a complex, adaptive, and fast-moving adversary category in which static signatures and monolithic classifiers fail to generalise under concept drift, evasion, and behavioural polymorphism. In this paper, we present Agentic SABRE (Semantic-Behavioural Arbitration for Ransomware Evaluation), an uncertainty-aware, neuro-symbolic, multi-agent framework for adaptive ransomware detection. SABRE fuses semantic, representation-based evidence with behavioural, time-window forensic telemetry and employs Monte Carlo Dropout inference to quantify epistemic uncertainty for each agent. We introduce a decision-layer orchestrator that performs risk- and uncertainty-aware triage using two interpretable thresholds: a risk score and an uncertainty budget. High-confidence, high-risk samples are automatically contained, while uncertain or borderline cases are escalated to human analysts, establishing a flexible computational contract between autonomous response and analyst oversight. To support auditability and trust, SABRE integrates post-hoc explainability mechanisms, including gradient saliency, permutation importance, and counterfactual analysis, enabling both local and global interpretation of agent decisions. Extensive evaluation on RDset and RanSMAP demonstrates that Agentic SABRE preserves perfect discrimination on saturated semantic datasets, with AUC equal to 1.0, while improving robustness under weak behavioural signals. It achieves up to a 4.9 percent relative reduction in false escalations at equal recall while maintaining calibrated predictive uncertainty. Counterfactual analysis further shows that semantic and behavioural decisions can be reversed with bounded perturbation cost, indicating stable and interpretable decision boundaries.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptive ransomware detection using runtime behavioral telemetry
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用运行时行为遥测进行自适应勒索软件检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用运行时行为遥测进行自适应勒索软件检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用运行时行为遥测进行自适应勒索软件检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a neuro-symbolic multi-agent framework combining semantic and behavioral evidence with uncertainty quantification for adaptive ransomware detection, improving robustness against evasion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对勒索软件行为多态性和概念漂移问题，提出Agentic SABRE框架，通过融合语义与行为证据并量化不确定性，实现自适应检测。实验表明该方法在抗规避和泛化方面优于传统方法，为动态恶意软件检测提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对勒索软件行为多态性和概念漂移问题，提出Agentic SABRE框架，通过融合语义与行为证据并量化不确定性，实现自适应检测。实验表明该方法在抗规避和泛化方面优于传统方法，为动态恶意软件检测提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: An Automated Framework for Generating Stealthy Cell-Embedded Hardware Trojans
quick_skim.abstract: Hardware Trojans (HTs) pose significant threats across the Integrated Circuit (IC) design lifecycle because they can be inserted by untrusted entities at different stages under the zero-trust model. When triggered under rare conditions, HTs can compromise the functionality, reliability, or security of the fabricated chip. HT assessment is typically performed by modeling realistic Trojan insertion scenarios in RTL implementation or gate-level netlists. While this model is useful for evaluating detection methods, it does not capture attacks where malicious behavior is hidden inside standard-cell implementations from a compromised library supplied by an untrusted vendor. This paper presents a novel framework for automatically generating cell-embedded hardware Trojans using compromised standard-cell implementations. Our proposed framework analyzes a mapped design, identifies candidate cell instances with rare input conditions, and applies payload templates that corrupt the selected cell output only when the trigger condition is satisfied. Experiments on open-source combinational and sequential benchmark designs show that our proposed framework can generate valid and stealthy Trojan instances across different cell types and design sizes. The results highlight a critical gap in current Trojan detection assumptions and show the need for cell-aware validation of standard-cell implementations in zero-trust IC design flows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hardware Trojan generation for security evaluation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于安全评估的硬件木马生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于安全评估的硬件木马生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于安全评估的硬件木马生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an automated framework for generating cell-embedded hardware Trojans, enabling more realistic security assessments of IC designs by hiding malicious behavior in standard-cell implementations, thus advancing hardware threat modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 硬件木马是集成电路安全的主要威胁之一，但现有评估方法多局限于RTL或门级建模，无法模拟攻击者通过受陷标准单元库注入的隐蔽恶意电路。本文提出一种自动化框架，在标准单元实现层自动生成隐蔽木马，支持多种触发条件和功能配置。该框架为硬件安全检测提供了更真实的测试输入，有助于提高芯片设计信任度，对保护异构系统中的硬件完整性具有参考价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 硬件木马是集成电路安全的主要威胁之一，但现有评估方法多局限于RTL或门级建模，无法模拟攻击者通过受陷标准单元库注入的隐蔽恶意电路。本文提出一种自动化框架，在标准单元实现层自动生成隐蔽木马，支持多种触发条件和功能配置。该框架为硬件安全检测提供了更真实的测试输入，有助于提高芯片设计信任度，对保护异构系统中的硬件完整性具有参考价值。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 系统安全领域相关文章，包括内核攻击防御、软件漏洞利用防御、软硬件分析测试、异构计算安全、可信执行环境/机密计算等, especially work that connects or combines: defense mechanisms for operating system kernels; static analysis of binary software for security; dynamic analysis techniques during runtime; security in heterogeneous computing environments; design and application of trusted execution environments; techniques for memory isolation in secure systems; How to defend against kernel level attacks in modern operating systems; Advanced techniques for software vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention; Security challenges in heterogeneous systems with CPUs and GPUs; Design principles of trusted execution environments for confidential computing.
